question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Http.IdleTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user open many keep-alive connections and hold them for the full Http.IdleTimeout duration, exhausting the server's file descriptor limit and preventing 30%+ of node-bound requests from being accepted?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Response.MaxTransactionsInBlock] Can an unprivileged external user exploit an unbounded Response.MaxTransactionsInBlock to request blocks containing millions of transactions, causing OOM conditions that crash the server and take 30%+ of node-processing goroutines offline?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Config.ShutdownTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a very long ShutdownTimeout to keep the server in a draining state for an extended period, during which 30%+ of network nodes receive no new transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Db.StatementTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Rosetta API request that triggers a DB query with no effective timeout (StatementTimeout=0), holding a DB connection indefinitely and blocking node-related data for 30%+ of concurrent processing threads?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Pool.MaxIdleConnections] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Pool.MaxIdleConnections set to zero to prevent connection reuse, forcing the server to open a new DB connection for every request and exhausting OS-level connection limits, starving 30%+ of node-processing paths?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Config.Online] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Online=false (offline mode) configuration path to cause the SDK client to be initialized without a mirror network address book, resulting in 30%+ of network nodes being unknown to the routing layer?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NodeMap key format (IP:port) by injecting entries with duplicate IPs but different ports, causing the SDK to treat multiple entries as the same node and effectively reducing the active node count below 70%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Cache.MaxSize] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a very large Cache.MaxSize to cause the entity cache to consume all available heap memory, triggering GC pressure that stalls 30%+ of node-processing goroutines?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Db.GetDsn()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the plaintext DSN produced by GetDsn() (sslmode=disable) to intercept DB credentials via network sniffing and then inject malicious node account IDs into the NodeMap, causing 30%+ of nodes to be routed to attacker-controlled endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Config.NodeRefreshInterval] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a very large NodeRefreshInterval (e.g., years) to prevent the SDK from ever refreshing the network address book, causing stale node data to persist and 30%+ of newly added nodes to never be discovered?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Pool.MaxLifetime] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Pool.MaxLifetime of zero (no expiry) to cause DB connections to persist indefinitely even after the DB server has closed them, causing silent connection failures that prevent 30%+ of node-related queries from succeeding?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Http.ReadTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user send HTTP requests with very large bodies slowly to exploit a missing or zero Http.ReadTimeout, tying up server goroutines and preventing 30%+ of node-destined construction requests from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Config.Network] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network string normalization (strings.ToLower) to inject a network name that resolves to an unexpected SDK network configuration, causing the SDK to use a node list where 30%+ of expected nodes are absent?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of validation on NodeMap entry count to inject a single-entry NodeMap, causing the SDK to route 100% of transactions through one node while 30%+ of other nodes receive zero traffic and are eventually marked inactive?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Db.StatementTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Rosetta /block request that triggers a complex DB join, and with StatementTimeout set to a very large value, hold the DB connection for minutes, blocking 30%+ of node-related data retrieval operations?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Config.Port] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Port field (uint16) by triggering a port conflict on the configured port, causing the HTTP server to fail to start and preventing all node-processing requests from being served, effectively shutting down 30%+ of node processing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Feature.SubNetworkIdentifier] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a SubNetworkIdentifier that does not match the configured shard/realm, causing the asserter to reject all requests destined for 30%+ of nodes that belong to the mismatched sub-network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Pool.MaxOpenConnections] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a low Pool.MaxOpenConnections value by sending concurrent requests that each hold a connection for the full StatementTimeout, causing a connection queue backlog that starves 30%+ of node-processing threads?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NodeMap's use of hiero.AccountID values to inject account IDs that map to non-existent or decommissioned nodes, causing the SDK to attempt connections to those nodes and marking 30%+ of them as unreachable?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Http.WriteTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a zero Http.WriteTimeout to cause the server to block indefinitely on response writes to slow clients, consuming all available goroutines and preventing 30%+ of node-bound transactions from being submitted?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Config.ShutdownTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated server restarts (e.g., by causing listen errors) combined with a very short ShutdownTimeout, causing in-flight node transactions to be aborted repeatedly and 30%+ of nodes to receive incomplete/failed submissions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Db.GetDsn()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sslmode=disable in GetDsn() to perform ARP poisoning on the local network, redirecting DB traffic to an attacker-controlled server that returns corrupted node account IDs, causing 30%+ of NodeMap entries to point to invalid nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Config.NodeRefreshInterval] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NodeRefreshInterval by triggering a scenario where the address book refresh returns an empty or partial node list, causing the SDK to remove 30%+ of nodes from its active routing table until the next refresh?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: Cache.MaxSize] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Cache.MaxSize that is too small relative to the number of active entities, causing constant cache eviction and repeated DB lookups that exhaust Pool.MaxOpenConnections and prevent 30%+ of node-processing queries from executing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NodeMap's lack of endpoint reachability validation at config load time to inject unreachable IP:port combinations, causing the SDK to waste connection attempts on 30%+ of configured nodes and marking them as failed?
